translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob and guided them, as We had guided Noah before them, and of his descendants, David and Solomon and Job and Joseph and Moses and Aaron. Thus We reward those who are upright and do good.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Ishaq (Isaac) and Yaqub (Jacob); We guided all of them; and We guided Nooh before them and of his descendants, Dawud and Sulaiman and Ayyub and Yusuf and Moosa and Haroon; and this is the way We reward the virtuous.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We gave to him Isaac and Jacob -- each one We guided, And Noah We guided before; and of his seed David and Solomon, Job and Joseph, Moses and Aaron -- even so We recompense the good-doers --

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Is-haq and Ya'qub: each one We guided. And Nuh We had guided afore, and of his progeny Da-ud and Sulaiman and Ayyub and Yusuf and Musa and Harun. And thus We recompense the well-doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Ishaque (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob), each of them We guided, and before him, We guided Nuh (Noah), and among his progeny Dawud (David), Sulaiman (Solomon), Ayub (Job), Yusuf (Joseph), Musa (Moses), and Harun (Aaron). Thus do We reward the good-doers.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob—each of them We guided. And We guided Noah previously; and from his descendants David, and Solomon, and Job, and Joseph, and Moses, and Aaron. Thus We reward the righteous.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We bestowed upon Abraham (offspring) Ishaq (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob) and each of them did We guide to the right way as We had earlier guided Noah to the right way; and (of his descendants We guided) Da'ud (David) and Sulayman (Solomon), Ayyub (Job), Yusuf (Joseph), Musa (Moses) and Harun (Aaron). Thus do We reward those who do good.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Ishaq and Ya`qub, each of them We guided, and before him, We guided Nuh and among his progeny Dawud, Sulayman, Ayyub, Yusuf, Musa, and Harun. Thus do We reward the good-doers.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Isaac and Jacob; each of them We guided; and Noah did We guide aforetime; and of his seed (We guided) David and Solomon and Job and Joseph and Moses and Aaron. Thus do We reward the good.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We gave him Isaac and Jacob and guided both; and We guided Noah before them, among his descendants were David and Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses and Aaron as such, We recompense the gooddoers,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We gave to Abraham, Isaac and Jacob - all [of them] We guided. And Noah, We guided before; and among his descendants, David and Solomon and Job and Joseph and Moses and Aaron. Thus do We reward the doers of good.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We gave (Abraham) Isaac and Jacob. Both had received Our guidance. Noah received Our guidance before Abraham and so did his descendants: David, Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses, and Aaron. Thus is the reward for the righteous people.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We gave to him Ishaq and Yaqoub; each did We guide, and Nuh did We guide before, and of his descendants, Dawood and Sulaiman and Ayub and Yusuf and Musa and Haroun; and thus do We reward those who do good (to others).

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We gave him Isaac and Jacob, each of whom We guided as We had guided Noah before. Among his descendants were David and Solomon, and Job, Joseph, Moses and Aaron. Thus do We reward the righteous.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We gave him Isaac and Jacob: all (three) guided: and before him, We guided Noah, and among his progeny, David, Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses, and Aaron: thus do We reward those who do good:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Zachariah and John We guided, and guided Jesus and Elias who were all among the upright.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And (We guided) Zakaria and Yahya (John) and Eisa and Elias; all these are worthy of Our proximity.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Zachariah and John, Jesus and Elias; each was of the righteous;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And also Zakariyya and Yahya and Isa and llyas: each one was of the righteous.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And Zakariya (Zachariya), and Yahya (John) and 'Iesa (Jesus) and Iliyas (Elias), each one of them was of the righteous.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Zechariah, and John, and Jesus, and Elias—every one of them was of the upright.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (And of his descendants We guided) Zakariya (Zachariah), Yahya (John), Isa (Jesus) and Ilyas (Elias): each one of them was of the righteous.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And Zakariyya, and Yahya and `Isa and Iliyas, each one of them was of the righteous.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And Zachariah and John and Jesus and Elias. Each one (of them) was of the righteous.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and (Prophets) Zachariah, John, Jesus and Elias, each was of the righteous,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And Zechariah and John and Jesus and Elias - and all were of the righteous.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We also gave guidance to Zacharias, John, Jesus, and Elias, who were all pious people,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Zakariya and Yahya and Isa and Ilyas; every one was of the good;

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Zachariah, John, Jesus, and Elijah, every one of them was righteous,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And Zakariya and John, and Jesus and Elias: all in the ranks of the righteous:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We gave guidance to Ishmael, Elisha and Jonah and Lot; And We favoured them over the other people of the world,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And Ismael (Ishmael) and Yasa’a (Elisha) and Yunus (Jonah) and Lut (Lot); and to each one during their times, We gave excellence over all others.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Ishmael and Elisha, Jonah and Lot-each one We preferred above all beings;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And Ismai'l and Alyas'a and Yunus and Lut: each one of them We preferred above the worlds.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And Isma'il (Ishmael) and Al-Yas'a (Elisha), and Yunus (Jonah) and Lout (Lot), and each one of them We preferred above the 'Alamin (mankind and jinns) (of their times).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Ishmael, and Elijah, and Jonah, and Lot—We favored each one of them over all other people.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (And of his descendants We guided) Isma'il (Ishmael), al-Yasa' (Elisha), Yunus (Jonah), and Lut (Lot). And each one of them We favoured over all mankind.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And Isma`il and Al-Yasa`, and Yunus and Lut, and each one of them We preferred above the `Alamin (mankind and Jinns, of their times).

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And Ishmael and Elisha and Jonah and Lot. Each one (of them) did We prefer above (Our) creatures,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and Ishmael, Elisha, Jonah and Lot. Each We preferred above the worlds,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And Ishmael and Elisha and Jonah and Lot - and all [of them] We preferred over the worlds.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: and Ishmael, Elisha, Jonah, and Lot whom We exalted over all people.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Ismail and Al-Yasha and Yunus and Lut; and every one We made to excel (in) the worlds:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Ishmael, Elisha, Jonah, and Lot -- We favoured each one of them above other people,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And Isma'il and Elisha, and Jonas, and Lot: and to all We gave favour above the nations:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Verily Jonah is one of the apostles.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And indeed Yunus is one of the Noble Messengers.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Jonah too was one of the Envoys;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And verily Yunus was Of the sent ones.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And, verily, Yunus (Jonah) was one of the Messengers.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Jonah was one of the messengers.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And Jonah too was one of the Messengers.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And verily, Yunus was one of the Messengers.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And lo! Jonah verily was of those sent (to warn)

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Jonah, too, was one of the Messengers.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And indeed, Jonah was among the messengers.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Jonah was certainly a Messenger.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Yunus was most surely of the apostles.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Jonah too was one of the messengers.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So also was Jonah among those sent (by Us).

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When he fled on the laden ship,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: When he left towards the laden ship.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: when he ran away to the laden ship

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Recall what time he ran away Unto a laden ship.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: When he ran to the laden ship,

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: When he fled to the laden boat.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Call to mind when he fled to the laden ship,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: When he ran to the laden ship:

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: When he fled unto the laden ship,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He ran away to the laden ship,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [Mention] when he ran away to the laden ship.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: He abandoned his people

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: When he ran away to a ship completely laden,

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He fled to the overloaded ship.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: When he ran away (like a slave from captivity) to the ship (fully) laden,

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And lots were cast (when a storm overtook them), he was rejected, (and thrown overboard).

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Then lots were drawn and he became of those who were pushed into the sea.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and cast lots, and was of the rebutted,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then he joined the lots, and was of the condemned.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He (agreed to) cast lots, and he was among the losers,

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He gambled and lost.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: cast lots, and was among the losers.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then he (agreed to) cast lots, and he was among the losers.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And then drew lots and was of those rejected;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and cast lots, and he was among the losers (of the lots that were cast).

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And he drew lots and was among the losers.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: and sailed away in a laden ship, wherein people cast lots. Because he lost, he was thrown into the water.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So he shared (with them), but was of those who are cast off.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: And then they cast lots and he was the one who lost,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He (agreed to) cast lots, and he was condemned:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Then he was swallowed by a large fish as he was worthy of blame.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: The fish then swallowed him and he blamed himself. (For not waiting for Allah’s command.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: then the whale swallowed him down, and he blameworthy.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And a fish swallowed him, and he was reproaching himself.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Then a (big) fish swallowed him and he had done an act worthy of blame.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then the fish swallowed him, and he was to blame.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Then a fish swallowed him, and he was blameworthy.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then a (big) fish swallowed him as he had done an act worthy of blame.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And the fish swallowed him while he was blameworthy;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: So the whale swallowed him, for he was blameworthy,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Then the fish swallowed him, while he was blameworthy.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: The fish swallowed him up and he deserved (all this).

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So the fish swallowed him while he did that for which he blamed himself

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and the fish swallowed him while he was blaming himself.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Then the big Fish did swallow him, and he had done acts worthy of blame.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Had he not been one of those who struggled hard,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And were he not one of those who praise. –

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Now had he not been of those that glorify God,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And had he not been of those who hallow Him,

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Had he not been of them who glorify Allah,

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Had he not been one of those who praised.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Had he not been one of those who glorify Allah,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Had he not been of them who glorify Allah,

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And had he not been one of those who glorify (Allah)

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and had he not been among those who exalt (Allah),

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And had he not been of those who exalt Allah,

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Had he not glorified God,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: But had it not been that he was of those who glorify (Us),

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Had he not been one of those who acknowledge the glory of God,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Had it not been that he (repented and) glorified Allah,

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He would have stayed in its belly till the day the dead are raised.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: He would have remained in its belly till the day when all will be raised.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: he would have tarried in its belly until the day they shall be raised;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He would have tarried in the belly thereof till the Day when they are raised.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He would have indeed remained inside its belly (the fish) till the Day of Resurrection.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He would have stayed in its belly until the Day they are raised.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: he would certainly have remained in its belly till the Day of Resurrection.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He would have indeed remained inside its belly (the fish) till the Day of Resurrection.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He would have tarried in its belly till the day when they are raised;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: he would have lingered in its belly till the Day they are resurrected.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He would have remained inside its belly until the Day they are resurrected.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: he would certainly have remained inside the fish until the Day of Resurrection.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He would certainly have tarried in its belly to the day when they are raised.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: he would certainly have remained inside the fish till the Day of Resurrection.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He would certainly have remained inside the Fish till the Day of Resurrection.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And (remember) Dhu'n-Noon (Jonah of the fish), when he went away in anger and imagined We will not test him (with distress). Then he called out from the darkness: "There is no god other than You. All glory to You; surely I was a sinner.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And remember Zun-Noon,* when he left in anger, assuming that We would not restrict him – he therefore called out in the realms of darkness, saying, “There is no God except You, Purity is to You; I have indeed committed a lapse.” (* Prophet Yunus – peace and blessings upon him)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And Dhul Nun -- when he went forth enraged and thought that We would have no power over him; then he called out in the darkness, 'There is no god but Thou. Glory be to Thee! I have done evil.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And Zun-nun! recall what time he departed in anger and imagined that We could not straiten him, then he cried in the darknesses, that: there is no god but Thou! hallowed be Thou! verily I have been of the wrong-doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And (remember) Dhan-Nun (Jonah), when he went off in anger, and imagined that We shall not punish him (i.e. the calamites which had befallen him)! But he cried through the darkness (saying): La ilaha illa Anta [none has the right to be worshipped but You (O Allah)], Glorified (and Exalted) are You [above all that (evil) they associate with You]. Truly, I have been of the wrong-doers."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Jonah, when he stormed out in fury, thinking We had no power over him. But then He cried out in the darkness, “There is no god but You! Glory to You! I was one of the wrongdoers!”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We bestowed Our favour upon Dhu al-Nun. Recall, when he went forth enraged, thinking We have no power to take him to task. Eventually he cried out in the darkness:" There is no god but You. Glory be to You! I have done wrong."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And (remember) Dhun-Nun, when he went off in anger, and imagined that We shall not punish him! But he cried through the darknesses (Zulumat) (saying): "There is no God but You, Glorified be You! Truly, I have been of the Zalimin wrongdoers."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And (mention) Dhu'n-Nun, when he went off in anger and deemed that We had no power over him, but he cried out in the darkness, saying: There is no Allah save Thee. Be Thou Glorified! Lo! I have been a wrong-doer.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And ThulNun (Prophet Jonah), he went away in anger thinking We had no power over him. But in the darkness he cried: 'There is no god except You. Exaltations to You! I was among the harmdoers'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [mention] the man of the fish, when he went off in anger and thought that We would not decree [anything] upon him. And he called out within the darknesses, "There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Dhun Nun went away in anger and thought that We would never have power over him, but in darkness he cried, "Lord, You are the Only God whom I glorify. I have certainly done wrong to myself (so forgive me)".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Yunus, when he went away in wrath, so he thought that We would not straiten him, so he called out among afflictions: There is no god but Thou, glory be to Thee; surely I am of those who make themselves to suffer loss.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Remember the man in the whale [Jonah] when he went away in anger, thinking We had no power over him. But he cried out in the darkness, "There is no deity but You. Glory be to You! I was indeed wrong."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And remember Zun-nun, when he departed in wrath: He imagined that We had no power over him! But he cried through the deptHs of darkness, "There is no god but thou: glory to thee: I was indeed wrong!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So wait with patience for the judgement of your Lord, and do not be like (onah) of the fish who called (to his Lord) when he was choked with anger.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Therefore wait for your Lord’s command, and do not be like the one of the fish; who cried out when he was distraught. (Prophet Yunus – peace be upon him.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: So be thou patient under the judgment of thy Lord, and be not as the Man of the Fish, when he called, choking inwardly.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Be patient thou, then, with thy Lord's judgment, and be thou not like him of the fish, when he cried out while he was in anguish.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So wait with patience for the Decision of your Lord, and be not like the Companion of the Fish, when he cried out (to Us) while he was in deep sorrow. (See the Quran, Verse 21:87).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: So wait patiently for the Decision of your Lord, and do not be like the Fellow of the Fish who cried out in despair.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: So bear with patience until the Judgement of your Lord comes, and do not belike the man in the fish (i.e., Jonah) who called out, choking with grief:

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So, wait with patience for the decision of your Lord, and be not like the Companion of the Fish when he cried out (to Us) while he was Makzum.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: But wait thou for thy Lord's decree, and be not like him of the fish, who cried out in despair.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: So be patient with the judgement of your Lord and do not be like the Companion of the Whale (Jonah), when he called out choking inwardly.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Then be patient for the decision of your Lord, [O Muhammad], and be not like the companion of the fish when he called out while he was distressed.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Exercise patience until the promise of your Lord (to punish the unbelievers) comes true. Do not be like Jonah (who left his people without the permission of God; he wanted them to be punished immediately),

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So wait patiently for the judgment of your Lord, and be not like the companion of the fish, when he cried while he was in distress.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Wait patiently for your Lord's judgement; do not be like the man who, having been swallowed by a whale, called out in distress.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So wait with patience for the Command of thy Lord, and be not like the Companion of the Fish,- when he cried out in agony.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Had it not been for a favour from his Lord he would have been cast blame-worthy on a barren plain.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Were it not for his Lord’s favour that reached him, he would have surely been cast onto the desolate land, reproached.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Had there not overtaken him a blessing from his Lord he would have been cast upon the wilderness, being condemned.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Had not grace from his Lord reached him, he had surely been cast into the wilderness in a plight.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Had not a Grace from his Lord reached him, he would indeed have been (left in the stomach of the fish, but We forgave him), so he was cast off on the naked shore, while he was to be blamed.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Were it not for his Lord’s favor that reached him, he would have been thrown into the wilderness, fully despised.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: had his Lord not bestowed His favour upon him, he would have been cast upon that barren shore (and would have remained there) in disgrace.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Had not a grace from his Lord reached him, he would indeed have been (left in the stomach of the fish, but We forgave him), so he was cast off on the naked shore, while he was to be blamed.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Had it not been that favour from his Lord had reached him he surely had been cast into the wilderness while he was reprobate.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Had the favor of his Lord not come upon him, he would have been blamed, cast upon the shore.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: If not that a favor from his Lord overtook him, he would have been thrown onto the naked shore while he was censured.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: and who cried (for help) to his Lord, while imprisoned and helpless inside the fish. Had it not been for a favor from his Lord, he would have been left out in the open, deserving blame for his shortcomings.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Were it not that favor from his Lord had overtaken him, he would certainly have been cast down upon the naked Found while he was blamed.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Had his Lord's grace not been bestowed upon him, he would have been cast away in disgrace upon that desolate shore.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Had not Grace from his Lord reached him, he would indeed have been cast off on the naked shore, in disgrace.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We heard his cry, and saved him from the anguish. That is how We deliver those who believe.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: We therefore heard his prayer and rescued him from grief; and similarly We shall rescue the Muslims.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: So We answered him, and delivered him out of grief; even so do, We deliver the believers.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: So We answered him, and We delivered him from the distress, and Thus do We deliver the believers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So We answered his call, and delivered him from the distress. And thus We do deliver the believers (who believe in the Oneness of Allah, abstain from evil and work righteousness).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: So We answered him, and saved him from the affliction. Thus We save the faithful.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Thereupon We accepted his prayer, and rescued him from grief. Thus do We rescue the believers.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So We answered his call, and delivered him from the distress. And thus We do deliver the believers.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Then we heard his prayer and saved him from the anguish. Thus we save believers.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We heard his prayer and saved him from grief. As such We shall save the believers.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So We responded to him and saved him from the distress. And thus do We save the believers.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We answered his prayer and saved him from his grief. Thus We save the faithful ones.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So We responded to him and delivered him from the grief and thus do We deliver the believers.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: So We heard his prayer and delivered him from sorrow. Thus shall We deliver the true believers.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So We listened to him: and delivered him from distress: and thus do We deliver those who have faith.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So We cast him, sick, on a barren shore,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: We then put him ashore on a plain, and he was sick.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: but We cast him upon the wilderness, and he was sick,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then We cast him on a bare desert whilst he was sick.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: But We cast him forth on the naked shore while he was sick,

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then We threw him into the wilderness, and he was sick.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: But We threw him on a wide bare tract of land while he was ill;

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: But We cast him forth on the naked shore while he was sick,

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Then We cast him on a desert shore while he was sick;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: But We cast him, upon the shore, and he was ill,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: But We threw him onto the open shore while he was ill.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We cast him out of the fish unto dry land and he was sick.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Then We cast him on to the vacant surface of the earth while he was sick.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: But We caused him to be cast forth on to the beach, sick as he was,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: But We cast him forth on the naked shore in a state of sickness,

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And We made a gourd tree grow over him.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We grew a tree of gourd (as a shelter) above him.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and We caused to grow over him a tree of gourds.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We caused to grow over him a tree, a gourd.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We caused a plant of gourd to grow over him.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We made a gourd tree grow over him.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and caused a gourd tree to grow over him,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We caused a plant of gourd to grow over him.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We caused a tree of gourd to grow above him;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and We caused a pumpkin tree to grow over him.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We caused to grow over him a gourd vine.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We made a plant of gourd grow up for him.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We caused to grow up for him a gourdplant.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and We caused a gourd tree to grow over him.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And We caused to grow, over him, a spreading plant of the gourd kind.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We sent him to a hundred thousand men or more,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We sent him towards a hundred thousand people, in fact more.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Then We sent him unto a hundred thousand, or more,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We had sent him to a hundred thousand: rather they exceeded.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We sent him to a hundred thousand (people) or even more.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then We sent him to a hundred thousand, or more.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and We sent him forth to a nation of a hundred thousand or more,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We sent him to a hundred thousand (people) or even more.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We sent him to a hundred thousand (folk) or more

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Then We sent him to a hundred thousand or more,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We sent him to [his people of] a hundred thousand or more.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We sent him to a hundred thousand or more people.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We sent him to a hundred thousand, rather they exceeded.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We sent him as a messenger to a hundred thousand people or more,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And We sent him (on a mission) to a hundred thousand (men) or more.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And they came to believe; so We allowed them to enjoy the good things of life for an age.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: So they accepted faith – We therefore gave them usage for a while.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and they believed; so We gave them enjoyment for a while.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And they believed, whrefore We let them enjoy life for a season.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And they believed; so We gave them enjoyment for a while.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And they believed, so We gave them enjoyment for a while.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and they believed. So We let them enjoy life for a while.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And they believed; so We gave them enjoyment for a while.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And they believed, therefor We gave them comfort for a while.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and they believed, and We gave them enjoyment for awhile.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And they believed, so We gave them enjoyment [of life] for a time.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They believed in him so We granted them enjoyment for an appointed time.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And they believed, so We gave them provision till a time.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and they believed in him: so We let them live in ease for a while.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And they believed; so We permitted them to enjoy (their life) for a while.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Why has there been no habitation that believed and profited by their faith, except the people of Jonah? When they came to believe, We removed from them the affliction of shame in the world, and made them prosperous for a time.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: So if only had there been one dwelling* that believed and its belief would have benefited it – except the nation of Yunus (Jonah)! When they accepted faith, We removed the disgraceful punishment in the life of this world from them, and let them enjoy for a while. (* That was destroyed after being warned.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Why was there never a city that believed, and its belief profited it? -- Except the people of Jonah; when they believed, We removed from them the chastisement of degradation in this present life, and We gave unto them enjoyment for a time.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Why then was there not a township which believed, so that its faith might have profited it, except the people of Yunus! When they believed, We removed from them the torment of humiliation in the life of the world, and We let them enjoy for a season.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Was there any town (community) that believed (after seeing the punishment), and its Faith (at that moment) saved it (from the punishment)? (The answer is none,) - except the people of Yunus (Jonah); when they believed, We removed from them the torment of disgrace in the life of the (present) world, and permitted them to enjoy for a while.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: If only there was one town that believed and benefited by its belief. Except for the people of Jonah. When they believed, We removed from them the suffering of disgrace in the worldly life, and We gave them comfort for a while.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Did it ever happen that the people of a town believed on seeing God's chastisement and its believing profited them? (There is no such instance) except of the people of Yunus. When they believed We granted them reprieve from humiliating chastisement in this world, and We let them enjoy themselves for a while.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Was there any town (community) that believed (after seeing the punishment), and its faith (at that moment) saved it (from the punishment)-- except the people of Yunus; when they believed, We removed from them the torment of disgrace in the life of the (present) world, and permitted them to enjoy for a while.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: If only there had been a community (of all those that were destroyed of old) that believed and profited by its belief as did the folk of Jonah! When they believed We drew off from them the torment of disgrace in the life of the world and gave them comfort for a while.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Why, was there never a village that believed and its belief benefited them? Except Jonah's nation, when they believed, We spared them from a degrading punishment in this life and We gave them enjoyment for awhile.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Then has there not been a [single] city that believed so its faith benefited it except the people of Jonah? When they believed, We removed from them the punishment of disgrace in worldly life and gave them enjoyment for a time.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Why did no one except the people of Jonah believe (in their punishment before their death) so that they could have benefitted from their faith? When the people of Jonah believed, We saved them from a disgraceful torment in this life and provided them with the means of enjoyment for an appointed time.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And wherefore was there not a town which should believe so that their belief should have profited them but the people of Yunus? When they believed, We removed from them the chastisement of disgrace in this world's life and We gave them provision till a time.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Why was there no other people, save the people of Jonah, who should have believed so that their belief would have benefited them. Once they believed, We lifted the torment of shame from them during their worldly life and let them enjoy Our provision for a while.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Why was there not a single township (among those We warned), which believed,- so its faith should have profited it,- except the people of Jonah? When they believed, We removed from them the penalty of ignominy in the life of the present, and permitted them to enjoy (their life) for a while.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We have sent revelations to you as We sent revelations to Noah and the prophets (who came) after him; and We sent revelations to Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac and Jacob, and their offspring, and to Jesus and Job, and to Jonah and Aaron and Solomon, and to David We gave the Book of Psalms,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Indeed We sent a divine revelation to you (Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessing be upon him) as We did send divine revelations to Nooh (Noah) and the Prophets after him; and We sent divine revelations to Ibrahim (Abraham) and Ismael (Ishmael) and Ishaq (Isaac) and Yaqub (Jacob) and their offspring, and Eisa (Jesus) and Ayyub (Job) and Yunus (Jonah) and Haroon (Aaron) and Sulaiman (Solomon), and We bestowed the Zaboor (the Holy Book) upon Dawud (David).

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: We have revealed to thee as We revealed to Noah, and the Prophets after him, and We revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes, Jesus and Job, Jonah and Aaron and Solomon, and We gave to David Psalms,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily We have revealed unto thee even as We revealed unto Nuh and the prophets after him, and as We revealed unto Ibrahim and Isma'il and Is-haq and Ya'qub and the tribes, and 'Isa and Ayyub and Yunus and Harun and Sulaiman, and unto Daud We vouchsafed a Scripture.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Verily, We have inspired you (O Muhammad SAW) as We inspired Nuh (Noah) and the Prophets after him; We (also) inspired Ibrahim (Abraham), Isma'il (Ishmael), Ishaque (Isaac), Ya'qub (Jacob), and Al-Asbat [the twelve sons of Ya'qub (Jacob)], 'Iesa (Jesus), Ayub (Job), Yunus (Jonah), Harun (Aaron), and Sulaiman (Solomon), and to Dawud (David) We gave the Zabur (Psalms).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: We have inspired you, as We had inspired Noah and the prophets after him. And We inspired Abraham, and Ishmael, and Isaac, and Jacob, and the Patriarchs, and Jesus, and Job, and Jonah, and Aaron, and Solomon. And We gave David the Psalms.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (O Muhammad!) We have revealed to you as We revealed to Noah and the Prophets after him, and We revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob and the offspring of Jacob, and Jesus and Job, and Jonah, and Aaron and Solomon, and We gave to David Psalms.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Verily, We have sent the revelation to you as We sent the revelation to Nuh and the Prophets after him; We (also) sent the revelation to Ibrahim, Isma`il, Ishaq, Ya`qub, and Al-Asbat, (the offspring of the twelve sons of Ya`qub) `Isa, Ayyub, Yunus, Harun, and Sulayman; and to Dawud We gave the Zabur.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Lo! We inspire thee as We inspired Noah and the prophets after him, as We inspired Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac and Jacob and the tribes, and Jesus and Job and Jonah and Aaron and Solomon, and as We imparted unto David the Psalms;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We have revealed to you as We revealed to Noah and to the Prophets after him, and We revealed to (Prophets) Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob and the tribes, Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon, and We gave to David the Psalms.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, We have revealed to you, [O Muhammad], as We revealed to Noah and the prophets after him. And we revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, the Descendants, Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon, and to David We gave the book [of Psalms].

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Muhammad), We have sent revelations to you just as were sent to Noah and the Prophets who lived after him and to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, his descendants, Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon. We gave the Psalms to David.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Surely We have revealed to you as We revealed to Nuh, and the prophets after him, and We revealed to Ibrahim and Ismail and Ishaq and Yaqoub and the tribes, and Isa and Ayub and Yunus and Haroun and Sulaiman and We gave to Dawood

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We have sent revelation to you [Prophet] as We did to Noah and the prophets who came after him, to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes, to Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon and David, to whom We gave the Psalms.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We have sent thee inspiration, as We sent it to Noah and the Messengers after him: we sent inspiration to Abraham, Isma'il, Isaac, Jacob and the Tribes, to Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and solomon, and to David We gave the Psalms.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (Remember) Ishmael, Edris and Dhu'l-Kifl. They were men of fortitude,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And remember Ismail, and Idrees, and Zul-Kifl; they were all patiently enduring.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And Ishmael, Idris, Dhul Kifl -- each was of the patient,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And Isma'il and ldris and Zul-Kifl! each were of the patient.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And (remember) Isma'il (Ishmael), and Idris (Enoch) and Dhul-Kifl (Isaiah), all were from among As-Sabirin (the patient ones, etc.).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Ishmael, and Enoch, and Ezekiel; each was one of the steadfast.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And (We bestowed the same favour) upon Ishmael, Idris and Dhu al-Kifl, for they were all steadfast.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And (remember) Isma`il, Idris and Dhul-Kifl: All were from among the patient.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And (mention) Ishmael, and Idris, and Dhu'l-Kifl. All were of the steadfast.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And Ishmael, Idris, and ThulKifl (the son of Job) each were patient.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [mention] Ishmael and Idrees and Dhul-Kifl; all were of the patient.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Ismael, Idris and Dhul Kifl all were people of great patience.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Ismail and Idris and Zulkifl; all were of the patient ones;

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Remember Ishmael and Idris and Dhul Kifl: they were all patient and steadfast.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And (remember) Isma'il, Idris, and Zul-kifl, all (men) of constancy and patience;

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And they were admitted to Our grace. Verily they were among the doers of good.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We admitted them into Our mercy; indeed they are among those who deserve Our proximity.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and We admitted them into Our mercy; they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We caused them to enter into our mercy, verily they are of the righteous.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We admitted them to Our Mercy. Verily, they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We admitted them into Our mercy. They were among the righteous.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We admitted them into Our mercy, for they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We admitted them to Our mercy. Verily, they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We brought them in unto Our mercy. Lo! they are among the righteous.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We admitted them to Our Mercy, for they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We admitted them into Our mercy. Indeed, they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We encompassed them in Our mercy; they were righteous people.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We caused them to enter into Our mercy, surely they were of the good ones.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We admitted them to Our mercy. They were all righteous men.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We admitted them to Our mercy: for they were of the righteous ones.

translation.author: Dr. Muhammad Muhsin
translation.text: Narrated 'Abdullah: The Prophet said, "None of you should say that I am better than Yunus (i.e. Jonah)." Musadded added, "Jonah bin Matta."

translation.author: Dr. Muhammad Muhsin
translation.text: Narrated Abu Huraira: The Prophet said, "Whoever says that I am better than Jonah bin Matta, is a liar."

translation.author: Dr. Muhammad Muhsin
translation.text: Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: The Prophet said that his Lord said: "It does not befit a slave that he should say that he is better than Jonah (Yunus) bin Matta.
